staticAssetModeReason: The current frontend intentionally uses same-origin /api and /static paths served by the Python backend.

runtimeResponsibilities: search runtime/backend target directories in packaged resources, portable package roots, environment overrides, and development paths
create the main window after backend readiness
terminate the backend process on app exit
productionBackend.binaryRole: start the local Starlette API and static web server

full.startup: desktop shell resolves runtime/backend and starts culvia-server

lite.role: desktop shell plus app-managed Python environment
lite.dependencyRule: detect modules with importlib.util.find_spec, create an app-managed venv when missing, and install culvia[desktop-runtime] into that venv

auto.role: prefer bundled backend, fall back to lite when no bundled backend exists

pythonRuntimeManager.globalPythonPolicy: Never install Culvia dependencies into a system/global Python environment; use an explicit virtualenv.

macosRuntimeSigning.reason: The bundled PyInstaller backend loads its unpacked Python runtime at process startup, so hardened runtime builds must disable library validation for that executable path.

checks: bundle.active and targets
bundle.resources includes runtime/backend
bundle.icon includes existing PNG, ICNS, and ICO platform outputs
main window create=false
optional backend binary executable
macOS Developer ID Application signing inputs
macOS notarization inputs
releasePreflight.credentialStorageRule: Use environment variables, keychain profiles, or CI secrets; never store Apple certificates, passwords, API keys, or notarization credentials in the repository.

macosAppPreflight.scope: local macOS app build only; strict Developer ID and notarization checks live in the notarized release lane
checks: macOS host
Xcode license accepted
cargo and npm available
desktop shell config parseable
ad-hoc, Apple Development, or Developer ID local signing identity
optional Apple Development identity visibility
macOS app artifact cleanup state is clean

macosAppCleanup.scope: local macOS app build outputs only

macosAppBuild.scope: local macOS app/dmg build and smoke; strict notarized release uses --strict-release-signing and --strict-artifacts
steps: optional cleanup of generated macOS app artifacts
desktop npm dependency install through project-local CLI
stage verified .app, .dmg, checksum, and evidence manifest under dist/macos

checks: unique .app and .dmg discovery
Info.plist matches desktop identifier and version
main executable and bundled backend in Contents/MacOS or Resources are executable Mach-O files
codesign verification for app, executable, and backend
Developer ID and hardened runtime signature detail checks
Gatekeeper assessment for app and DMG
hdiutil DMG verification
stapler validation for DMG
lipo architecture reporting for executable and backend
launch the built .app Contents/MacOS executable
force the desktop shell to use the bundled backend
inject isolated fixture photo/cache environment
extend CULVIA_DESKTOP_READY_TIMEOUT_SECS for frozen backend startup
extend CULVIA_DESKTOP_BACKEND_HEALTH_TIMEOUT_SECS for frozen backend self-health
extend CULVIA_DESKTOP_FRONTEND_READY_TIMEOUT_SECS for webview DOM readiness
wait for backendReady smoke event after backend health passes
wait for windowCreated smoke event after the main desktop window is created
wait for frontendReady smoke event after the webview loads the workbench DOM
verify runtime API state, web asset, thumbnail, manual mark, export preflight, selected export, and curation history through the .app-started backend
auto-exit the app only after frontendReady or frontendReadyTimeout has been emitted

Linux.folderPicker: zenity or kdialog directory picker when installed
Linux.reveal: xdg-open or gio open parent directory

nativeFileActions.fallbackRule: If the platform command is unavailable, /api/capabilities disables the native action and the web UI keeps manual path input or directory upload available.
nativeFileActions.securityBoundary: Reveal requests are still authorized by the Python app against the current source/cache state before invoking the platform file manager.

secureCredentialStorage.sqlitePolicy: SQLite may persist LLM base URL, endpoint, model, input mode, prompt preset, and custom prompt, but must never persist the full API key.
secureCredentialStorage.automationSafety: CULVIA_DISABLE_KEYCHAIN=1 disables keychain reads/writes for smoke tests and CI fixtures.
secureCredentialStorage.runtimeSmokePolicy: Must be run explicitly in a real desktop user session; default release gates must not write to system keychain without --allow-write.

checks: safe archive paths with exactly one top-level directory
no runtime cache, database, CSV, upload, thumbnail, model cache, or build artifacts
Windows zip layout and PE machine validation
Linux tar.gz layout, ELF validation, executable bits, and launcher runtime wiring
manifest kind, launcher, backend, web entry, relative paths, and mustNotRequireUserPython
reuse portable package artifact preflight
extract the final archive into a temporary directory
resolve launcher, bundled backend, and web entry from manifest relative paths
start the packaged launcher with CULVIA_DESKTOP_SMOKE=1
wait for backendReady, windowCreated, and frontendReady smoke events
run fixture curation/export workflow through the launcher-started backend
require native Windows/Linux runners for launch smoke

requiredSteps: install xvfb on Linux runners for headless desktop launcher smoke
build frozen PyInstaller backend on the target runner
smoke the frozen backend through ready JSON and /health
run desktop shell build on the target runner
extract the final package and smoke the packaged desktop launcher
run formal package gate with the verified archive
write a SHA-256 checksum next to the verified archive
write a release evidence manifest after every required step has passed
desktopReleaseWorkflow.artifactUploadRule: Upload only the final zip/tar.gz archive, its .sha256 checksum, and its .evidence.json manifest after artifact preflight, runtime verification, formal package gate, checksum, and evidence manifest pass; never upload target, dist directories, backend binaries, caches, runtime state, credentials, or user data.
desktopReleaseWorkflow.credentialRule: Windows/Linux portable package jobs use no secrets and only require read-only repository permissions.

checks: aggregate Desktop readiness and desktop release workflow contract
summarize explicit pip wheel/install and sdist release smoke evidence
summarize macOS app/dmg artifact preflight evidence
summarize Windows zip and Linux tar.gz artifact status
summarize release checksum and evidence manifest status for Windows/Linux artifacts
summarize explicit system keychain smoke evidence
list formal release blockers and next commands
split blockerSummary into localActionable, externalRequired, and environment groups
split estimatedRemaining into localActionable and externalRelease so external release evidence is not counted as local development rounds
split nextCommandsByCategory so release owners can run local smoke before external runner work
write public-redacted JSON release status evidence for release archival
return non-zero in strict mode when formalReady is false

firstStageReleaseArtifacts.goal: Self-contained desktop packages that do not require a system Python installation.
firstStageReleaseArtifacts.pythonRuntimePolicy: Build one PyInstaller onedir backend runtime per target OS/architecture and bundle it inside the desktop package. Model weights remain cache/download data, not bundled executable payload.

windows.shape: zip with culvia-desktop.exe, runtime/backend/<target>/culvia-server/, share/culvia/web, and manifest.json

macOS.shape: macOS .app containing the bundled runtime/backend/<target>/culvia-server directory

linux.shape: tar.gz with bin/culvia launcher, bin/culvia-desktop, runtime/backend/<target>/culvia-server/, share/culvia/web, and manifest.json

forbidden: Do not duplicate scoring, filtering, LLM review, curation, cache, or export logic in the desktop shell layer.
Do not store API keys in source-controlled desktop shell config, JSON contract files, or SQLite plaintext.
Do not switch to bundled static frontend assets until /api and /static base URLs are explicitly abstracted and tested.
openBuildBlockers: The PyInstaller backend binary has been smoke-tested on the current macOS ARM64 development machine; every release target OS still needs its own build and smoke test.
Platform signing/notarization and installer verification still need real release-environment execution.
